title: senecio bertramii
intro: Senecio bertramii, also known as Bertram's ragwort, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, particularly its hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and protecting against liver damage. Senecio bertramii can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used to treat various health issues, including liver and gallbladder problems, as well as to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress.
